_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to identify intertextual references in literary texts and explain how they create new layers of understanding.

_source.w: We are learning to identify intertextual references in literary texts and explain how they create new layers of understanding.
y: Intertextuality is how texts speak to each other across time and culture.
Recognising these connections enriches our reading and reveals deeper layers of meaning.
This skill is essential for understanding how literature participates in broader cultural conversations.
eg: We identify a reference to a myth, fairy tale or earlier literary work within a text we are studying.
We explain how an intertextual reference adds meaning that would be missed without recognising the source.
We discuss how a modern retelling of a classic story creates new meanings through its references.
We analyse how allusion — a brief reference to another text — works to enrich a literary passage.
We compare a text with its source material and discuss how the intertextual relationship shapes meaning.
We create a text that deliberately uses intertextual references to enhance its meaning.
